reasoning: The rule requires that when a TLS 1.3 server receives a ClientHello outside the initial handshake, it MUST terminate with an 'unexpected_message' alert. The enforcement happens at the state machine transition layer: (1) ossl_statem_server_read_transition checks SSL_CONNECTION_IS_TLS13(s) and delegates to ossl_statem_server13_read_transition. (2) In ossl_statem_server13_read_transition, when in state TLS_ST_OK (post-handshake), only SSL3_MT_KEY_UPDATE and certificate messages are accepted; SSL3_MT_CLIENT_HELLO is not in the allowed transitions, so the function returns 0. (3) Back in ossl_statem_server_read_transition, the failure leads to SSLfatal(s, SSL3_AD_UNEXPECTED_MESSAGE, SSL_R_UNEXPECTED_MESSAGE) at line 312, which is the correct 'unexpected_message' alert. (4) SSL3_AD_UNEXPECTED_MESSAGE maps directly to the 'unexpected_message' alert as confirmed by ssl_stat.c. Additionally, tls_process_client_hello has a safety assertion ossl_assert(!SSL_CONNECTION_IS_TLS13(s)) at the renegotiation check, confirming that TLS 1.3 post-handshake ClientHellos should never reach message processing — they are rejected at the transition layer with the correct alert.

evidence.expected_behavior: Server must send 'unexpected_message' alert when receiving ClientHello after TLS 1.3 handshake completion
